prerequisites: A Lightspeed Retail (X-Series) account on a plan that supports personal tokens. Per the API docs, personal tokens are only available to retailers on the Plus plan.
Admin access to the account, since personal tokens are created by admin users directly within the Lightspeed web application.
Your store domain prefix (subdomain). This is the prefix in your account URL and forms the API base URL https://<subdomain>.retail.lightspeed.app/api/.
A personal token (used as the api_key) generated from the account, to authorize API requests.

authMethods.name: Personal token (Bearer) via api_key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single api_key, which is a Lightspeed Retail (X-Series) personal token. The token is attached to every request in the Authorization header as a Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer <token>), used just like an OAuth access token. Personal tokens are created by admin users in the Lightspeed web application and require the Plus plan.

setupSteps.title: Confirm plan and admin access
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Lightspeed Retail (X-Series) account as an admin. Personal tokens are created by admin users within the web application and, per the API documentation, are only available to retailers on the Plus plan.

setupSteps.title: Generate a personal token
setupSteps.body: Create a personal token from your Lightspeed account. This token is a bearer credential and is used just like an OAuth token, attached to every request in the Authorization header. Copy the token value and store it securely, as it is the api_key the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Find your subdomain (domain prefix)
setupSteps.body: Identify your store domain prefix from your account URL. It is the subdomain used to build the API base URL: https://<subdomain>.retail.lightspeed.app/api/. For example, a domain prefix of mystore yields https://mystore.retail.lightspeed.app/api/.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the personal token as api_key and your store domain prefix as subdomain. Both fields are required. The connector sends api_key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header against the API base URL derived from subdomain.
